questions.question: Causative form: “I had my car {a1}.”
Informal causative: “She got her phone {a1}.”
Use causative for services: “We had the house {a1}.”
The standard phrase is “I had my hair {a1}.”
“I got the car repaired” means someone else {a1} it.
“I repaired my car” means I did it myself; “I had my car repaired” means someone else {a1}.
Negative causative: “I {a1} my car serviced last year.”
Question form: “Did you {a1} your eyes tested?”
Future causative: “We will have the windows {a1} tomorrow.”
Do not write “I had my car to repair”; write “I had my car {a1}.”
“They finally got the roof {a1}” stresses the result after effort.
